source_quote: Training compute for frontier LMs grows ~5x/year; frontier physical compute ~3.8x/year adjusted for hardware price-performance.
justification_summary: Central frontier compute-growth prior (asi.txt Q2), anchored to Epoch trends.

source_quote: Compute needed for fixed LM performance halves every ~8 months (95% CI 5-14), i.e. ~3x/year effective.
justification_summary: Foundational pre-training efficiency (asi.txt Q3); excludes transient test-time gains.

source_quote: 50% time-horizon doubling ~7 months (2019-2024); a fit to 2024-2025 models gives ~3.5-4 months.
justification_summary: METR time-horizon trend; 7mo mode is the long-run rate, not the recent steeper one.

source_quote: 9 seconds (GPT-3, 2020) to 14.5 hours (Claude Opus 4.6, Feb 2026), roughly doubling every 7 months.
justification_summary: Mode 8h is conservative vs the 14.5h Feb-2026 point estimate; wide range 2-40h.

source_quote: SWE-bench Pro is the uncontaminated standard; ~85% resolution ~= strong economic automation.
justification_summary: WEAK: hours proxy pending a SWE-bench-Pro-percent to time gate (deferred).

source_quote: Code enables tool-use and self-scaffolding; lag ~ half a training generation.
justification_summary: AGGRESSIVE compression of the coding->generality lag (asi.txt Q7).

source_quote: Frontier lab statements imply nonzero deployment/workflow-integration delays even when models are capable.
justification_summary: Operational integration lag before broad AGI-level use.

source_quote: AI-driven code tasks at Anthropic double in length every ~4 months; Sakana AI Scientist runs full ML research loops overnight with ~3.5h human oversight.
justification_summary: STRESS-TEST at the steep end of observed research-loop acceleration; NOT a direct point estimate (perplexity #3). Baseline alternative = 12 months.

source_quote: No source states '2-3 months to superhuman'; framed as aggressive extrapolation from ~4-month task-horizon doublings and overnight research pipelines.
justification_summary: STRESS-TEST fast-takeoff scenario (perplexity #3). Baseline alternative = 12 months.

source_quote: Aggressive FTM distribution: 20%->100% R&D automation window ~2.3-4.2 years, almost fully overlapping the AGI year.
justification_summary: AGGRESSIVE takeoff (asi.txt Q10 / FTM aggressive branch). Main FTM branch is ~6 years.

source_quote: Grid interconnection queues commonly 36-60 months; switchgear lead times 40-60 weeks.
justification_summary: WELL-SOURCED physical floor on internal ASI (asi.txt Q12, high confidence).

source_quote: Overlapping 20-100% R&D and economic-automation windows cover well over half of their union (~0.5-0.8 band).
justification_summary: ~two-thirds overlap of the cognitive automation epoch (perplexity #2).

source_quote: Tier-1 US labs bypass strict export controls via National Validated End User exemptions; domestic power permitting is the main friction.
justification_summary: Minimal internal-capability friction (asi.txt Q14).

source_quote: RSP v3.0 removed unconditional hard pauses; safety testing / red-teaming imposes ~3-12 month pre-release delays.
justification_summary: Public deployment review delay (public ASI only).

source_quote: National-security classification of a frontier ASI plausibly withholds public visibility 6-24 months.
justification_summary: Public-visibility delay (does not slow internal capability).

source_quote: METR implies a near-unit elasticity (~10x effective compute -> ~10x horizon); we down-weight to 0.5 (10x -> ~3.2x).
justification_summary: CONSERVATIVE robustness prior below METR's implied elasticity (perplexity #1). NOTE: engine exponent semantics differ from asi.txt Q1.

source_quote: sigma = [ln(14)-ln(2)]/2.5631 = 0.76 from p10=2028, p90=2040 (superforecaster/expert fat tail).
justification_summary: Long-tail width fitted to external distributions (asi.txt Q13).

source_quote: ~45% compute, ~45% algorithms, ~10% data (CV); >75% of algorithmic progress is compute-augmenting -> positive coupling.
justification_summary: Moderate POSITIVE prior; below the near-unity coupling implied by effective-compute framing (perplexity #5).

source_quote: Near-linear relation between log(effective FLOP) and log(task horizon): faster compute growth compresses the doubling time.
justification_summary: MECHANICAL negative (faster compute -> SMALLER doubling-months). This is NOT the compute-vs-algo -0.70 stress-test in perplexity #5.

source_quote: Algorithmic efficiency (~3x/year) shortens the effective doubling time alongside compute.
justification_summary: MECHANICAL negative (faster algo -> smaller doubling-months).

source_quote: 2023 ESPAI aggregate (gamma CDF, 1714 responses): 50% chance of HLMI by 2047 (down from 2060 in 2022).
justification_summary: EXTERNAL benchmark; the model's ~2031 AGI is ~16 years EARLIER than the ESPAI HLMI median.

source_quote: 2023 ESPAI (774 responses): 50% chance of full automation of labor by 2116.
justification_summary: EXTERNAL benchmark for full automation; far later than the model.
EXTERNAL benchmark; the model's ~2031 AGI sits at Bio Anchors' 10% (early) edge, not its median.
